root_text.iti7:2.1: “Sabbaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.iti7:2.2: Sabbañca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ tattha cittaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.
root_text.iti7:4.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti7:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti7:2.1: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding the all, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.iti7:2.2: By directly knowing and completely understanding the all, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”
translation_text.iti7:2.3: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.iti7:3.1: “Those who know the all as all,
translation_text.iti7:3.4: and have risen above all suffering.”
translation_text.iti7:4.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti7:3.3: sabbapariññā → sabbaṁ pariññāya (bj); sabbaṁ pariññā (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.iti7:3.4: sabbadukkhamupaccagā”ti → sabbaṁ dukkhaṁ upaccagāti (sya-all); sabbadukkhaṁ upaccagāti (pts-vp-pli1)
